2026-06-17-v1.49-narrative-indexes.title: Intermittent flaky test `fallback_warn_includes_chapter_field` (review_report) fails under full parallel `cargo test -p nexus-orchestration`; passes in isolation. Pre-existing on iteration/v1.49 base.
2026-06-17-v1.49-narrative-indexes.source: P1 implementer (pre-existing flake, verified against base per .mstar/AGENTS.md protocol); QC3 also verified on origin/main @ be27111b
2026-06-17-v1.49-narrative-indexes.note: Verified pre-existing per the repo's 'Pre-existing claim verification protocol': reproduces on iteration/v1.49 @ 3630a4e5 (base) with `cargo test -p nexus-orchestration` (full parallel suite — 9 passed, 1 failed in review_report); passes with `cargo test -p nexus-orchestration --test review_report` (in isolation, parallel and serial). QC3 also verified on origin/main @ be27111b with 2/10 failure rate. Cause: global tracing registry contention when many test binaries install/inspect the subscriber concurrently. Not caused by V1.49 P1 — review_report.rs and its capture layer are untouched. Suggested fix: scoped subscriber guard or serializing that single test.

2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.title: TZ silently clobbered on role-only patch operations (data consistency)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.source: qc1 (architecture/maintainability) W1; corroborated by qc2/qc3 (no disagreement on the data-loss path)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.note: Trigger: `set my-work --no-review` (or any patch omitting --tz) on a Work with custom TZ stored. handle_set folds env/default TZ into effective_args.tz unconditionally, then apply_set_args patches schedule.tz = resolved_tz, clobbering the previously-configured author TZ. Suggested fix: only fold env/default TZ when user explicitly resets (no-flags → defaults branch) or passes --tz; for patch operations without --tz leave base.tz unchanged. Recommend extracting TZ resolution into a pure helper (resolve_tz) for testability. Add regression test set_no_review_preserves_custom_tz.
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.closure_note: V1.50 T-A P0 fix-wave: `resolve_tz(args_tz, env) -> Option<String>` pure helper extracted; `handle_set` only folds env/default TZ on explicit reset or --tz; for role-only patch operations, base.tz is preserved. Regression test `set_no_review_preserves_custom_tz` passes; 3 unit tests cover the pure helper.

2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.title: Spec §3.1 'all-off' rule not enforced (spec compliance)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.scope: crates/nexus42/src/commands/creator/works/cron.rs :: CronCommand::Set (no --all-off flag) and apply_set_args (no all-disabled check)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.note: Spec §3.1 verbatim: 'At least one role must remain enabled: true unless --all-off is passed (CLI rejects empty schedules).' Both --all-off flag and post-mutation check missing. `set my-work --no-brainstorm --no-write --no-review` succeeds silently. T-A P1 cron evaluator will then skip all fires indefinitely. Fix: add --all-off flag in CronCommand::Set clap; post-mutation count of enabled roles; if 0 and !all_off return CliError::Config with stable code (e.g. E_CRON_ALL_ROLES_DISABLED). Hermetic test apply_set_args_all_off_without_flag_rejects.
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.closure_note: V1.50 T-A P0 fix-wave: `--all-off` flag added to `CronCommand::Set` clap definition; `apply_set_args` post-mutation check rejects all-disabled with stable code `E_CRON_ALL_ROLES_DISABLED`. `apply_set_args_all_off_without_flag_rejects` and `..._with_flag_succeeds` tests pass. `cron set --help` documents --all-off. Cross-corroborated by qc1 (W2) and qc3 (W-001) re-review.

2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.title: Spec §3.2 show output missing Local time + Next fire (UTC) columns (spec completeness)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.note: Spec example mandates 4 columns: Role / Cron / Local time / Next fire (UTC). Implementation renders only Role / Cron / Enabled. The whole point of per-Work cron config is 'let authors set the work on autopilot' — show is the primary verification surface. Fix: Local time = parse hour/minute fields of cron expression + render in TZ + cap at 3 entries with …; Next fire (UTC) = cron::Schedule::from_str(&normalize_cron_fields(expr)).upcoming(Utc).next() (single .next() per role, O(1)). render disabled when !enabled. Hermetic test asserts the two new columns.
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.closure_note: V1.50 T-A P0 fix-wave: 4-column `render_show` with Role / Cron / Local time / Next fire (UTC) columns. Single `.next()` per role (O(1)) for next-fire UTC; `local_time_display` parses min/hour fields; disabled state renders `--` / `disabled`. `render_show_includes_local_and_next_fire_columns` and `render_show_disabled_renders_disabled_for_next_fire` tests pass.

2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.title: list_works_schedule unbounded — workspace-scale performance risk
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.note: SELECT work_ref, work_id, schedule_json FROM works WHERE creator_id = ? AND workspace_slug = ? ORDER BY updated_at DESC has no LIMIT. For N ~ 5000 Works (multi-author workspace) latency grows linearly and CLI output becomes unreadable. Existing list_works precedent uses WorkListFilters::limit/offset (default 100). Fix: add optional limit param on list_works_schedule (default 100), pass through handle_list via --limit <N> flag. Update unit test to assert the cap.
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.closure_note: V1.50 T-A P0 fix-wave: `limit: Option<u32>` parameter (default 100) added to `list_works_schedule` DAO; `LIMIT ?` SQL bound; `--limit <N>` clap flag in `CronCommand::List`. `list_works_schedule_applies_limit` (DAO) + `cron_list_help_documents_limit_flag` (CLI) tests pass.

2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.title: handle_set read-modify-write TOCTOU window (reliability, P0 single-user small risk; P1 daemon-writer introduces real race)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.target: V1.50 T-A P1 (daemon writer race window)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.note: handle_set: get_schedule_json → apply_set_args → set_schedule_json. No transaction. T-A P1 will introduce a daemon-side cron writer (spec §4) — that writer will be the racing party. P0 single-user CLI risk is microsecond window. Fix at P1: extract set_schedule_json_tx (atomic SELECT-then-UPDATE-with-version) OR CAS via UPDATE ... WHERE schedule_json = ? retry on mismatch. P0 fix-wave adds in-code TODO marker at cron.rs:613. Plan-level residual carried to T-A P1.
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.closure_note: V1.50 T-A P1: `set_schedule_json_tx` transactional CAS implemented; `handle_set` (and the cron evaluator) use the atomic variant. 4 hermetic tests cover rejection on stale preimage, concurrent-writer serialisation, successful apply on matching preimage, and missing-work error. In-code TODO marker removed.

2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.title: novel-write embedded preset not yet authored (defer to T-A P2)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.scope: embedded-presets/novel-writing/ (novel-write preset absent); cron evaluator enqueues the correct preset_id per spec but executor needs the preset authored
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.note: Evaluator contract is complete and tested (18 cron_supervisor tests). novel-brainstorm works end-to-end. novel-write schedule persists + admits normally but the executor body is missing — author the embedded-presets/novel-writing/<write-stage>.yaml in T-A P2. This is a preset-authoring workstream, not an evaluator defect.

2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.title: Full-workspace `cargo clippy --all` deferred to CI per `target/` hygiene policy
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.note: Touched crates (nexus-orchestration, nexus-local-db, nexus42, nexus-daemon-runtime) verified clean locally; full-workspace clippy is the authoritative CI gate per .mstar/AGENTS.md `target/` hygiene rule. Accept (CI verifies).

2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.title: cron_fires_at_minute re-parses the cron expression per (Work, role) per tick (perf hot path)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.note: At 1000 Works × 3 roles/min this is ~30ms/tick of avoidable CPU. Suggested fix: parse once on set_schedule_json write (store normalized form) OR memoise per-Work using a small in-process HashMap<(work_id, role), Schedule> cache. Acceptable for V1.50 scale (60s tick budget absorbs); fix in this fix-wave for low-risk surgical change.
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.closure_note: V1.50 T-A P1 fix-wave: `OnceLock<Mutex<HashMap<(work_id, role), (raw, Schedule)>>>` memoisation cache with content-drift invalidation; pub `invalidate_cron_schedule_cache` called from CLI CAS-write path. Test `cron_fires_at_minute_uses_memoised_schedule` passes (100 calls = 1 parse).

2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.title: idempotency-skip log line at info-level → too noisy at scale (tracing volume)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.note: 100 Works on * * * * * = 12k info lines/hour. One-line fix: downgrade to debug!. Aggregated sweep summary line at line 146 already covers observability.
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.closure_note: V1.50 T-A P1 fix-wave: one-line change `info!` → `debug!` at cron_supervisor.rs:263 with 5-line justification comment. Manual source verification; sweep summary line covers the counter (no automated test for log level).

2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.title: novel-brainstorm / novel-write fall through to _ => 1 in preset_version_for_id; no test guard (preset-version drift surface)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.scope: crates/nexus-orchestration/src/auto_chain.rs:1451-1468 (preset_version_for_id fallback); :2127-2174 (sync test array omits new cron preset ids)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.note: When T-A P2 authors novel-write (R-V150P1CRONBW-01) and bumps version, cron-fired schedules will be silently stamped preset_version=1 and treated as stale. Fix: add match arms for novel-brainstorm + novel-write in preset_version_for_id; extend preset_version_mapping_matches_yaml sync test array. Failure mode is silent but cheap to fix now.
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.closure_note: V1.50 T-A P1 fix-wave: explicit match arms for novel-brainstorm + novel-write in preset_version_for_id; sync test renamed + extended; 2 new tests pass. `novel-write` preset YAML still deferred (R-V150P1CRONBW-01) but the sync test now tolerates its absent YAML and will auto-enforce strict sync once authored.

2026-06-18-v1.50-kb-editor-cli.scope: nexus42/CLI: author gate for edit/delete resolves via world ownership not works ownership
2026-06-18-v1.50-kb-editor-cli.note: KeyBlocks are World-scoped (entity-scope-model §5.1.1) and kb_key_blocks has no works FK. The owner_creator_id resolution is the canonical ownership path per entity-scope-model §1.2. Reads (list/show) are local-first (no gate); edit/delete gate on world owner. Edit re-runs ValidationMode::Novel via SqliteKbStore::with_validation_mode.

2026-06-18-v1.50-kb-auto-promotion.note: Heuristic (capitalized noun phrases) cannot distinguish character/location/organization. Authors correct on adopt via `creator world kb adopt`. LLM extraction is compass §0.1 OUT (defer V1.51+). Accept at P1; not blocking.

2026-06-18-v1.50-kb-auto-promotion.title: resolve_workspace_id reads narrative_gateway.workspace_id which may be stale; column is informational only (defer to T-B P2)
2026-06-18-v1.50-kb-auto-promotion.note: T-B P2 refreshable scan will use the freshest workspace_id from the chapter frontmatter; resolve_workspace_id staleness self-corrects in T-B P2.

2026-06-18-v1.50-kb-auto-promotion.title: kb_adopt not wrapped in a single SQLite transaction (data integrity gap; recoverable orphan)
2026-06-18-v1.50-kb-auto-promotion.scope: crates/nexus42/src/commands/creator/world/kb.rs:472-488 (kb_adopt: insert_key_block + mark_confirmed as two non-atomic operations)
2026-06-18-v1.50-kb-auto-promotion.note: Failure modes: (1) insert succeeds, mark_confirmed returns Err → orphan KeyBlock + still-pending promotion row; (2) insert succeeds, mark_confirmed returns Ok(false) → misleading error message + orphan. Fix: wrap both calls in pool.begin() transaction; if either fails, ROLLBACK. The current hermetic test double_adopt_is_rejected does NOT cover the race between insert_key_block and mark_confirmed — add a regression test that mocks mark_confirmed failure to assert the orphan is not created.
2026-06-18-v1.50-kb-auto-promotion.closure_note: V1.50 T-B P1 fix-wave: kb_adopt now does pool.begin() → insert_key_block_in_tx + mark_confirmed_in_tx → explicit tx.rollback() on !flipped or error before any commit. Misleading error message replaced with 'The transaction was rolled back; no orphan row created.' New regression test kb_adopt_failure_rolls_back_insert pre-flips the candidate and asserts zero orphan KeyBlock rows after rollback. Cross-corroborated by qc2 (security) + qc3 (reliability) re-review.

2026-06-18-v1.50-kb-auto-promotion.title: idx_kb_extract_jobs_promotion_status_work covers (status, work_id) but list_pending_for_world query filters on (status, world_id) — index unused for documented purpose
2026-06-18-v1.50-kb-auto-promotion.note: Index does not cover the dominant list path (which filters on world_id). For V1.50 scale (10-100 pending rows per world) this is sub-millisecond and not a hot path, but the index is functionally unused. Fix: add idx_kb_extract_jobs_promotion_status_world (promotion_status, world_id, created_at); remove or rename the unused index. Migration comment also needs correction.
2026-06-18-v1.50-kb-auto-promotion.closure_note: V1.50 T-B P1 fix-wave: DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_kb_extract_jobs_promotion_status_work; CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_kb_extract_jobs_promotion_status_world ON kb_extract_jobs (promotion_status, world_id, created_at). Trailing created_at gives index-ordered scan with no filesort. Migration comment fully rewritten. New regression test pending_list_uses_world_id_covering_index uses EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN to assert the planner picks the new world_id index.

2026-06-18-v1.50-kb-auto-promotion.title: reject audit log path uses internal work_id under Works/<work_id>/... instead of documented work_ref (path convention drift)
2026-06-18-v1.50-kb-auto-promotion.note: Spec §5.5.4 mandates retention in Logs/kb/rejected/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<extract_job_id>.md. Current code resolves work_id (numeric internal PK) and writes to Works/<work_id>/...; should resolve work_ref (user-facing identifier) and write to Works/<work_ref>/Logs/kb/rejected/. Fix: use work_ref not work_id for the filesystem path; validate work_ref exists before write.
2026-06-18-v1.50-kb-auto-promotion.closure_note: V1.50 T-B P1 fix-wave: kb_reject calls resolve_work_ref_for_log before mark_rejected. Helper returns Err (and aborts) if work_id missing, works row absent, or story_ref NULL. write_rejected_log now takes work_ref and writes under Works/<work_ref>/Logs/kb/rejected/ (log body still records both work_id for audit and work_ref). 2 new regression tests: kb_reject_writes_log_under_work_ref_path + kb_reject_fails_when_work_ref_missing.

2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.title: novel-write embedded preset not yet authored (defer to T-A P2)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.scope: embedded-presets/novel-writing/ (novel-write preset absent); cron evaluator enqueues the correct preset_id per spec but executor needs the preset authored
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.note: Evaluator contract is complete and tested (18 cron_supervisor tests). novel-brainstorm works end-to-end. novel-write schedule persists + admits normally but the executor body is missing — author the embedded-presets/novel-writing/<write-stage>.yaml in T-A P2. This is a preset-authoring workstream, not an evaluator defect.

2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.title: Full-workspace `cargo clippy --all` deferred to CI per `target/` hygiene policy
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.note: Touched crates (nexus-orchestration, nexus-local-db, nexus42, nexus-daemon-runtime) verified clean locally; full-workspace clippy is the authoritative CI gate per .mstar/AGENTS.md `target/` hygiene rule. Accept (CI verifies).

2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.title: cron_fires_at_minute re-parses the cron expression per (Work, role) per tick (perf hot path)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.note: At 1000 Works × 3 roles/min this is ~30ms/tick of avoidable CPU. Suggested fix: parse once on set_schedule_json write (store normalized form) OR memoise per-Work using a small in-process HashMap<(work_id, role), Schedule> cache. Acceptable for V1.50 scale (60s tick budget absorbs); fix in this fix-wave for low-risk surgical change.
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.closure_note: V1.50 T-A P1 fix-wave: `OnceLock<Mutex<HashMap<(work_id, role), (raw, Schedule)>>>` memoisation cache with content-drift invalidation; pub `invalidate_cron_schedule_cache` called from CLI CAS-write path. Test `cron_fires_at_minute_uses_memoised_schedule` passes (100 calls = 1 parse).

2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.title: idempotency-skip log line at info-level → too noisy at scale (tracing volume)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.note: 100 Works on * * * * * = 12k info lines/hour. One-line fix: downgrade to debug!. Aggregated sweep summary line at line 146 already covers observability.
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.closure_note: V1.50 T-A P1 fix-wave: one-line change `info!` → `debug!` at cron_supervisor.rs:263 with 5-line justification comment. Manual source verification; sweep summary line covers the counter (no automated test for log level).

2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.title: novel-brainstorm / novel-write fall through to _ => 1 in preset_version_for_id; no test guard (preset-version drift surface)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.scope: crates/nexus-orchestration/src/auto_chain.rs:1451-1468 (preset_version_for_id fallback); :2127-2174 (sync test array omits new cron preset ids)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.note: When T-A P2 authors novel-write (R-V150P1CRONBW-01) and bumps version, cron-fired schedules will be silently stamped preset_version=1 and treated as stale. Fix: add match arms for novel-brainstorm + novel-write in preset_version_for_id; extend preset_version_mapping_matches_yaml sync test array. Failure mode is silent but cheap to fix now.
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-brainstorm-write.closure_note: V1.50 T-A P1 fix-wave: explicit match arms for novel-brainstorm + novel-write in preset_version_for_id; sync test renamed + extended; 2 new tests pass. `novel-write` preset YAML still deferred (R-V150P1CRONBW-01) but the sync test now tolerates its absent YAML and will auto-enforce strict sync once authored.
